features.name: Mechanical Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Table-stakes for any hospital exit product. NFPA 101 and DIN EN 179/1125 make audible alarm mandatory. Safety (9): fail-safe at 0V for elopement detection. Reliability (9): mechanical linkage eliminates electrical failure.

features.name: Key Override Mechanism
features.rationale: Hospitals require authorized bypass during code blue, psychiatric handoffs, fire drills without triggering facility-wide alarm. Stress (8): nurse/security confidence in authorized access. Safety (8): prevents inappropriate escalation.

features.name: Pre-Alarm Warning Tone
features.rationale: Absent from mechanical-only competitors. Clinical exits have high incidental contact rates. Pain (8): eliminates false alarm call-out burden. Stress (8): reduces alarm fatigue — a documented patient safety risk.

features.name: Wireless State Transmitter
features.rationale: More reliable coverage = more value. Directly addresses N08 (OS=18): undetected activation during overnight. Safety (8): real-time replaces hours-later discovery. Reliability capped at 6 due to C3 and C6.

features.name: Battery Power Supply
features.rationale: No-mains-wiring retrofit is a practical necessity for occupied hospital wards. Cost (7): zero electrician spend for 200+ door retrofit. Safety (8) + Reliability (8): alarm uptime during power failures.

features.name: Remote Battery Status Reporting
features.rationale: Absent from all mechanical-only competitors. Manual battery checks across 200+ hospital doors is a documented burden. Pain (8): eliminates manual rounds. Delight (8): facilities directors genuinely surprised.

features.name: Retrofit Surface Mount Installation
features.rationale: More retrofit simplicity = more value. Wired alternatives require 2-4 hrs/door with licensed electricians. Skill (9): facility tech replaces specialized trades. Pain (9): eliminates dominant retrofit barrier.

features.name: Automated Event Log Generation
features.rationale: Highest scoring feature. JCI compliance documentation is chronically underserved (N16 OS=16, N20 OS=14). Stress (9): eliminates pre-survey documentation scramble. Confidence (9): 12-month audit trail on demand.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of wireless signal attenuation below reliable threshold in reinforced-concrete hospital floor construction
Minimize the likelihood of wireless coverage gaps discovered post-installation requiring unplanned repeater infrastructure
Minimize the likelihood of alarm event transmission failures caused by RF interference from MRI and CT imaging equipment
Minimize the effort required to replace batteries across all campus exit devices within a planned maintenance window
Minimize the likelihood of installation failure on non-standard door hardware configurations in pre-1990 hospital wings
Minimize GDPR compliance exposure from door event logs containing data attributable to identifiable individuals
Minimize the integration effort to relay wireless alarm events from EasyWave into hospital BACnet or nurse-call BMS platforms
